User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user (visitor or admin) of the UniTrack Campus Visitor system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's institutional email address, used for login and identification.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'admin', 'visitor').

isBlocked.description: A flag indicating if the user's account is currently blocked from accessing the system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Visit.title: Visit
Visit.description: Records a single visit by a user to the campus, capturing details about their presence.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Visit entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this visit. (Relationship: User 1:N Visit)

checkInTime.description: Timestamp when the user checked into the campus.

checkOutTime.description: Timestamp when the user checked out of the campus. Null if the visit is still active.

collegeDepartment.description: The specific college department the user visited during this particular visit.

reasonForVisit.description: The reason specified by the visitor for this specific visit.

UserSession.title: UserSession
UserSession.description: Tracks active user sessions for real-time monitoring on the admin dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this session. (Relationship: User 1:N UserSession)

loginTime.description: Timestamp when the user logged in and this session began.

logoutTime.description: Timestamp when the user logged out and this session ended. Null if the session is still active.

deviceId.description: An identifier for the device from which the user logged in (e.g., unique device ID, browser session ID).

isActive.description: A flag indicating whether the user session is currently active.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is typically restricted to the owner (matching `userId` with `request.auth.uid`). Admins may have additional permissions for user management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, derived from `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains active and inactive user sessions. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. This collection is readable by administrators for the real-time dashboard (using queries on 'isActive'), and writable by the session's owner.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user session.

definition.description: Records individual campus visits. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. This collection is readable by administrators for statistics and activity logs, and writable by the visit's owner.

params.name: visitId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific campus visit.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to store administrative roles. The existence of a document at this path for a given `adminId` signifies that the user is an administrator, enabling database-backed access control (DBAC).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, derived from `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to fulfill the real-time 'Active Sessions' dashboard requirement for administrators while adhering to strict security and scalability principles. We utilize top-level collections for 'UserSession' and 'Visit' entities (`/user_sessions/{sessionId}` and `/visits/{visitId}`) to enable efficient querying and real-time listening for administrative dashboards. Each document in these collections includes a denormalized `userId` field to achieve Authorization Independence. This allows security rules to evaluate access based solely on the document's own data (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) without requiring expensive or non-atomic `get()` operations to parent documents or related user profiles. This strategy is critical for supporting atomic operations like session creation/updates and ensures robust security rules that are easy to debug.

To support QAPs (Query As Permission) for the admin dashboard, the `user_sessions` collection is structured to be readable by any administrator and filterable by the `isActive` status. Similarly, the `visits` collection is globally readable by administrators and filterable by various criteria (department, reason, classification). This allows administrators to perform `list` operations directly on these collections, applying `where` clauses (e.g., `where('isActive', '==', true)` or specific filters for `visits`) to retrieve relevant real-time data efficiently. The security rules will grant read access to these collections only to authenticated users whose `uid` exists in the `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access the dashboard data. Individual users will have write access only to documents where `resource.data.userId` matches their `request.auth.uid`.

Structural Segregation is applied by keeping user profiles in `/users/{userId}`, administrative roles in `/roles_admin/{adminId}`, and session/visit data in separate, top-level collections (`/user_sessions/{sessionId}` and `/visits/{visitId}`). This ensures that each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture: `/users` for private user data, `/roles_admin` for global role checks, and `/user_sessions` and `/visits` for user-owned data that is also admin-viewable, making rules clear and predictable. The `isActive` flag in `UserSession` and explicit timestamps (`checkOutTime`, `logoutTime`) provide clear state modeling, enhancing data clarity and predictability.
